summary: The diff implements the frontend FuelSalesLog page, CSV export utility, backend data model, routing, and sidebar navigation. However, the backend enforces authentication (AccessControl checks) on both createSaleRecord and getAllSaleRecords, contradicting the no-authentication requirement. The banner image is not present in the diff. The frontend page imports auth-related hooks suggesting possible login gating. The visual theme is partially verifiable from color class references in the truncated diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: No authentication is required to call any backend function. Records must survive canister upgrades with no data loss.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend createSaleRecord and getAllSaleRecords functions both check AccessControl.hasPermission and trap with Unauthorized if the caller lacks the #user permission, contradicting the no-auth requirement. Additionally fuelSaleRecords is declared as var with no visible stable keyword, raising concern about upgrade persistence.

missing_requirements.requirement: No login or authentication is required to access or submit the form.
missing_requirements.reason: FuelSalesLog.tsx imports useInternetIdentity and useGetCallerUserProfile (auth hooks), suggesting the page may conditionally block access or show a login prompt for unauthenticated users, violating the no-auth constraint.

missing_requirements.requirement: No login or authentication is required to view the sales history.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend getAllSaleRecords query requires the caller to have #user permission, so unauthenticated frontend calls would be rejected, preventing the history from loading for unauthenticated users.

hallucinated_features.description: Authentication and access-control guards added to createSaleRecord and getAllSaleRecords backend functions
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request explicitly states no login or authentication is required and all features must be accessible without signing in. The backend nevertheless enforces AccessControl permission checks on both fuel sale functions.

hallucinated_features.description: LogIn icon imported and likely used in FuelSalesLog to show a login prompt
hallucinated_features.reason: The FuelSalesLog page imports a LogIn icon from lucide-react alongside useInternetIdentity, suggesting an authentication-gated UI path was added despite the no-login requirement.
